bull.b: Sustained TTM operating cash flow and strong free cash flow growth materially reduce Alnylam's dependence on external financing. bull.t: Strong TTR Commercial Franchise And Adoption
bull.b: Rapid TTR revenue growth, expanding prescriber base and high real-world adherence reflect durable clinical and payer acceptance. A deep, sticky commercial franchise in rare disease supports predictable recurring revenue, facilitates lifecycle extension and strengthens negotiating leverage with payors and partners over

bear.t: Rising Royalty Pressure On Gross Margins
bear.b: Higher, sales-linked royalty rates create a structural headwind to product-level gross margins as volumes grow. This recurring margin pressure reduces operating leverage from top-line growth, compresses free cash flow conversion and can materially affect long-term profitability unless offset by price, mix or lower COGS

bear.t: Moderate Leverage And Prior Equity Weakness
bear.b: Elevated leverage and a history of very low equity constrain financial flexibility and raise sensitivity to profit deterioration or higher rates. While improved, the balance sheet can quickly tighten if earnings reverse, increasing refinancing and covenant risk and limiting capital allocation optionality for strategic

bear.t: Rising Operating Investments And Revenue Variability
bear.b: Steep increases in R&D and SG&A raise the break-even revenue threshold and amplify execution risk if commercial growth lags.
